hypothesis: If combining pre-training on large chemical databases with domain-specific fine-tuning and reinforcement learning guided by expert-defined physicochemical reward functions is applied to generative models, then the resulting configurations will produce novel, valid, and diverse CTF structures satisfying expert-defined material criteria at higher rates than any single knowledge transfer strategy alone, because the sequential application of broad chemical knowledge, domain adaptation, and task-specific optimization provides complementary inductive biases that progressively constrain the chemical space toward viable porous carbon materials for supercapacitors.
